popularStory.headline: Great Memorial
popularStory.description:
~MY ANCESTOR~
The son of HENRY & ALICE (KING) BALDWIN, he married JANE WELLES/WILLIS on Sept. 28, 1590 in Cholesbury, Bucks, Eng.
He was mentioned in the will of his grandmother (ELLEN APUKE) BALDWIN …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sylvester Baldwin於1560年9月28日在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓受洗時，他的父親Henry Baldwin31歲，母親Alice King28歲。他於1590年9月28日與Jane Welles在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾爾斯波利結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1632年6月1日在英國巴金哈姆爾爾斯波利享年71歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭貝德福爾特拉爾 貝德福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Baldwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’, which was extremely popular among the Normans and in Flanders in the early Middle Ages. It was the personal name of the Crusader who in 1100 became the first Christian king of Jerusalem, and of four more Crusader kings of Jerusalem. It was also borne by Baldwin, Count of Flanders (1172–1205), leader of the Fourth Crusade, who became first Latin Emperor of Constantinople (1204). In North America, this surname has absorbed Dutch forms such as Boudewijn.
Irish: surname adopted in Donegal by bearers of the Gaelic surname Ó Maolagáin (see Milligan ), due to association of Gaelic maol ‘bald, hairless’ with English bald.
History: A John Baldwin from Buckinghamshire, England, arrived in the US in 1638 and settled in Milford, CT.

variants.name: Milligan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolagáin ‘descendant of Maoileagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of maol meaning ‘bald’ or ‘tonsured’.

variants.name: Baldovino
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Philippines and Colombia) and Italian: from the personal name Baldovino, from ancient Germanic Baldowin (see Baldwin ).

variants.name: Bellmann
meanings: North German:
habitational name for someone from Belle in Westphalia, Bell in the Rhineland, or Bellen near Bremen.
in Saxony, from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal name   Baldwin  .

variants.name: Bais
meanings: Dutch (mainly North Holland): patronymic from the personal name Baije, which is a derivative of an ancient Germanic name beginning with the element bern- ‘bear’, or possibly a variant of Boije, which may be a derivative of Boidin and thus a variant of Baldwin .
Italian (Friuli Venezia Giulia and Trentino-South Tyrol): nickname derived from German weiss ‘white’.

Americanized form of Croatian and Slovenian Bajs: nickname from dialect bajs ‘bass’ (the musical instrument), figuratively ‘fatty, potbelly’. The surname in the form Bais is found in Croatia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Baudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Etienne, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Firmin, Francoise, Jean-Yves, Laurent, Minos, Sylvain.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Boudoin 1 and Bowdoin .
Altered form of French   Beaudoin  , a cognate (variant) of 1 above.

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Balkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Herschel.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish female personal name Beyle meaning ‘beautiful’ (related to French belle).

English (of Norman origin): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Baldekin, Baudekin, an Anglo-Norman borrowing of Middle Dutch Baudequin, a Flemish pet form of Baldwin . However, a clear link between Middle English Baudekin and post-medieval Balkin has not been established, and the modern name may have a different source, a variant of Balcom perhaps, by migration.

variants.name: Bawden
meanings: English: from a late variant of the Norman personal name   Baldwin   or a variant of   Bowden  .

variants.name: Baldon
meanings: English: habitational name from either Marsh Baldon or Toot Baldon in Oxfordshire, from the Old English personal name Bealda + dūn ‘hill’, or a variant of Baldwin . Occasionally the name may be from Baildon in Yorkshire (named with Old English bēgel ‘bend’ + dūn ‘hill’; recorded as Baldon in the 16th century), or from Ballidon in Derbyshire (named with Old English belg ‘bag, sack’ + denu ‘valley’).
English: variant of   Boldon  .
Italian (Veneto): from an augmentative of the personal name   Baldo  .

variants.name: Beaudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Serge, Jacques, Gilles, Laurent, Normand, Pierre, Alcide, Lucien, Raoul.

French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Baudoin , Baudouin , Beaudion , Boadway , Boardway , Bodeau , Bodoh , Bodway , Boudway , and Budway .

nameMeanings.name: Sylvester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Silvester .

variants.name: Silvester
meanings: From a Latin name, meaning ‘of the woods’. It was borne by various early saints, most notably by the first pope to govern a Church free from persecution (  314–35 ). His feast is on  31 December , and in various parts of Europe the New Year is celebrated under his name. The name has been continuously, if modestly, used from the Middle Ages to the present day.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Sylvester Baldwin was christened on 28 September 1560, in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Baldwin, was 31 and his mother, Alice King, was 28. He married Jane Welles on 28 September 1590, in Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 1 June 1632, in Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Central Bedfordshire, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sylvester Baldwin a été baptisé le 28 septembre 1560, à Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry Baldwin, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Alice King, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Jane Welles le 28 septembre 1590, à Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 1 juin 1632, à Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Central Bedfordshire, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lui sposato Jane Welles il 28 settembre 1590, nel Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Sylvester Baldwinが1560年9月28日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアアストン クリントンで洗礼を受けた時，父Henry Baldwinは31歳，母Alice Kingは28歳であった。1590年9月28日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアチオレスブリーでJane Wellesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1632年6月1日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアチオレスブリーで71歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアセントラル ベドフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sylvester Baldwin(이)가 1560년 9월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Baldwin(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice King(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1590년 9월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 컬스버이에서 Jane Welles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1632년 6월 1일 영국 벅잉함실르 컬스버이에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 세트럴 베드퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sylvester Baldwin foi batizado em 28 de setembro de 1560, em Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry Baldwin, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Alice King, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Welles em 28 de setembro de 1590, em Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de junho de 1632, em Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Central Bedfordshire, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sylvester Baldwin был крещен 28 сентября 1560 г. в местности астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry Baldwin было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Alice King, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Welles 28 сентября 1590 г. в местности чолесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 1 июня 1632 г. в местности чолесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности кентраль бедфордшир, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sylvester Baldwin se bautizó el 28 de septiembre de 1560, en Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry Baldwin, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Alice King, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Jane Welles el 28 de septiembre de 1590, en Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de junio de 1632, en Cholesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Central Bedfordshire, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
